title: Race-held-out cross-fitting for every high-leakage feature
summary: The current best code uses random StratifiedKFold and builds some statistics on concatenated train+test data.
rationale: The current best code uses random StratifiedKFold and builds some statistics on concatenated train+test data. In this task, rows from the same Year/Race share strong context, so random folds can overstate AUC. scikit-learn explicitly recommends group-based holdouts when groups encode experiments or time-like collections, and CatBoost’s ordered-boosting paper motivates leakage-safe categorical statistics.
implementation_hint: Use `groups = Year + '__' + Race` and compare `GroupKFold` or `LeaveOneGroupOut` against the current random CV. Recompute frequency encodings, target encodings, and same-lap aggregates inside each fold from training rows only. Select features/blend weights by a robust score such as mean(random AUC, grouped AUC) or the minimum of the two.
expected_effect: Lower local CV is possible, but leaderboard robustness should improve and any gains from new features will be more trustworthy.
risk: May expose that some current gains are transductive rather than generalizable, so short-term CV could drop before improving.
